title: Ski Resort Lift Traffic: Skier-Segment Clustering
isolation: candidate runs as an isolated stdin->stdout subprocess via isorun; true segments and hidden data stay in the evaluator process
strategies: rank-quantile split of the first usage feature into k contiguous groups (reproduces the evaluator baseline -> ~0.1 on every instance)
Lloyd k-means on z-standardized features (k-means++ init, restarts): strong on compact/blob/high-dim segments but slices interleaving crescents and concentric rings through the middle

normalized spectral clustering on a self-tuning kNN affinity graph (k-means on row-normalized Laplacian eigenvectors): recovers non-convex crescents/rings AND compact/anisotropic/unequal-spread/high-dim segments -> high geometric mean across the whole family

other viable designs: agglomerative single/average-linkage cut to k, Gaussian-mixture EM (recovers anisotropy/unequal spread), DBSCAN-style density with label reconciliation to k, mutual-kNN connected components
